channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Supreme Court of Canada to Hear B.C. Appeal on Indigenous Mineral Rights under DRIPA
item.description: The Supreme Court of Canada will hear the B.C. government’s appeal regarding a landmark case on Indigenous mineral rights and the application of the province’s Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous ...

item.title: Dutch Navy Ship HNLMS De Ruyter Arrives in the Philippines to Strengthen Bilateral Maritime Cooperation
item.description: In a significant development for Philippine-Dutch relations, a Dutch navy ship has made a visit to the Philippines after several years of absence. This arrival is expected to boost the maritime partne...

item.title: Upcoming Events and Activities in Ottawa from May 22 to 24
item.description: This article provides a guide to events and activities happening in Ottawa over the weekend of May 22 to 24, 2026. Residents and visitors can explore a wide range of cultural, recreational, and commun...

item.title: Karachi Court Places Anmol 'Pinky' Under Judicial Custody in 18 Cases Involving Drugs and Murder
item.description: KARACHI: A Karachi court has placed Anmol, also known as Pinky, under judicial remand in all 18 cases she faces, following her production in multiple criminal proceedings. Pinky, allegedly involved in...

item.title: Trump Shares Outdated Front Page Amid Declining Approval Ratings
item.description: President Donald Trump posted a three-month-old New York Post front page to Truth Social, praising himself for his 'winning' economic message in a February State of the Union address. The post, captio...

item.title: Cristiano Ronaldo ends five-year trophy drought as Al-Nassr wins Saudi Pro League
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo has ended his five-year wait for a major club trophy as Al-Nassr secured the 2025-26 Saudi Pro League title with a 4-1 win against Damac. The victory was powered by goals from Sadio ...

item.title: Allegations of sexual assault on reality TV highlight real-world risks and consent issues

item.title: The Artistic Legacy of Joseph Karl Stieler, Bavarian Court Painter

item.title: Parents of Southport Attack Survivors Speak Out About Challenges of Anonymity
item.description: Parents of children who survived the Southport knife attack in July 2024 have expressed concern that the court-imposed anonymity for their daughters has led to a lack of support and public recognition...

item.title: Four People Missing After Boat Capsizes in Lamu Channel
item.description: Four people went missing while four others were rescued after a boat capsized in Lamu. The incident happened near the Manda-Bruno channel on Wednesday night at around 9pm. The boat, carrying eight peo...

item.title: A Comprehensive Look at the Marx Brothers' Career in the 1930s and 1940s

item.title: Kenya Meteorological Department Forecasts Rain Across Four Regions Until May 26

item.title: The Life and Legacy of Mary Mallon: America's First Identified Asymptomatic Typhoid Carrier
item.description: Mary Mallon, an Irish immigrant born in 1869, arrived in the United States in 1883 and became a skilled cook for affluent families in New York City. Unbeknownst to her, she was the first person in the...

item.title: A 1899 Photograph Captures a Couple Sharing a Tender Moment in a Pumpkin Patch
item.description: This 1899 photograph depicts a young couple sharing a quiet kiss in a pumpkin patch, offering a glimpse into personal life at the turn of the 20th century. The image captures both intimacy and histori...

item.title: Prince Rahim Aga Khan visits Gilgit-Baltistan for community engagements
